discovery.run_summary: Mixed tape with VIX up ~7% despite SPY +0.23% — risk-on in semis/energy (Hormuz/oil concerns, AI rally) but selling in communication services, staples, and regional banks. Earnings reactions dominate the mid-cap movers, with several notable bundled 8-K filings (SHAK in particular) and a striking +22% breakout in Vicor with no visible catalyst.

notable_index_moves: VIX +6.92% to 18.38 alongside SPY green — hedging demand rising despite index strength
XLE +2.64% and oil-sensitive names rallying on Hormuz/Iran ceasefire deterioration
SMH +1.72%, NVDA +1.97% — semis leading risk appetite
KRE -1.86% — regional banks weak, possibly tied to KKR/FSK private credit headline
XLC -1.16% with GOOGL -3.03% dragging on AI hacking story
XLP -0.96% — staples weak, ELF and CELH consistent with consumer discretionary/staples wobble

discoveries.name: Vicor Corporation
discoveries.setup: Large breakout with no visible 8-K or scheduled earnings catalyst
discoveries.thesis: VICR is a power-management chip supplier with AI datacenter exposure (48V power delivery for GPU racks). +22% on no SEC filing and modest 2x volume looks like either a private news leak (customer win, design-in disclosure at a conference) or aggressive AI-power sympathy buying piggybacking on NVDA/SMH strength. The 5-day change of only 24.7% means almost all of this is today.
discoveries.what_confirms: A press release or industry-conference disclosure naming a hyperscaler design win, or a sell-side upgrade citing AI power architecture share gains; sustained volume on day 2.
discoveries.what_kills: No follow-on news within 48 hours and the move fades back below $290 — suggests a single-day momentum/short-squeeze with no fundamental driver. Empty 8-K slate at this magnitude is suspicious.
discoveries.what_to_learn: When a small-float component name moves 20%+ without an 8-K, check trade-show calendars and analyst day schedules before assuming squeeze — power-semis often pop on private customer disclosures that don't trigger filings.
discoveries.catalyst: Unknown — no 8-K, no earnings; possibly AI-power sympathy or unfiled customer-related news
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: catalyst_signals is empty {} — no filings, no earnings within window; classification must remain UNCLEAR despite the magnitude.
research_pointers: Check Vicor IR / press releases for the last 48 hours
Check OCP Summit, Hot Chips, or hyperscaler events this week
Look at options flow — call skew and IV blowout vs. realized would suggest event-driven vs. squeeze
Short interest as % of float — VICR has historically had elevated SI

discoveries.name: Shake Shack, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Earnings reaction with bundled 5.02 officer departure plus 2.02 results in same window
discoveries.thesis: Two 8-Ks filed 5/7: one with 2.02 earnings, the other with 5.02 officer departure. The combination of an earnings print AND a management change in the same filing window is a textbook bad-news bundle. With the stock already down 32.5% over 5 days, today's -8% likely reflects continued institutional re-rating rather than fresh news. Magnitude appears justified given dual catalysts.
discoveries.what_confirms: 5.02 exhibit reveals abrupt CFO or COO departure (not pre-announced retirement), guide-down on same-store sales in the earnings deck, sell-side downgrades cascading over next 2 days.
discoveries.what_kills: 5.02 turns out to be planned board succession or a controlled CEO transition with a strong replacement named — then the -32% 5-day move is overdone and a bounce candidate.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Multi-8K days that pair 2.02 (earnings) with 5.02 (officer change) almost always justify magnitude — companies rarely volunteer two bad events simultaneously unless forced; the 5-day cumulative move is usually the better read than the single day.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Separate 8-K with 5.02 (departure) filed same day as the earnings 8-K — bundling pattern that typically signals genuine fundamental deterioration.
research_pointers: Read the 5.02 exhibit — identify role, replacement, severance/comp terms
Check same-store sales guide vs Street and vs prior quarter
Compare to CAVA, CMG, WING reactions for sector context
Look for any 10b5-1 sales by insiders in trailing 60 days

discoveries.name: Whirlpool Corporation
discoveries.setup: Continuing post-earnings selloff compounded by weak housing data
discoveries.thesis: WHR reported 5/6 (2.02). Stock down 22.6% over 5 days and another -8.6% today — today's leg likely tied to the April home sales miss reported this morning (CNBC). Appliances are tightly correlated to existing-home turnover. Compounding catalyst, magnitude looks justified.
discoveries.what_confirms: Housing starts and existing home sales prints over next 2 weeks confirm slowdown; WHR cuts FY guide further; dividend coverage questions surface.
discoveries.what_kills: Mortgage rates pull back sharply on Fed/Iran resolution; April housing data revised; WHR announces meaningful tariff offset or restructuring.
discoveries.catalyst: Post-earnings selloff + April home sales disappointment
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: 2.02 earnings 5/6 plus today's CNBC housing data tying directly to WHR's end market.
research_pointers: Read WHR earnings deck — tariff exposure commentary and FX
Check dividend yield vs payout ratio — already stressed at sub-$3B cap
Cross-reference with LZB, MHK, GIL reactions

discoveries.name: Installed Building Products
discoveries.setup: Earnings reaction compounded by housing-data sympathy
discoveries.thesis: Reported 5/7 with 2.02. Stock now down 25.6% over 5 days. Today's -6% likely pure housing-data sympathy (April home sales miss). The cumulative move is large for what is typically a high-quality compounder; magnitude reflects builder slowdown but watch for overshoot if housing data stabilizes. Worth a closer look than SHAK or WHR because IBP has a stronger historical track record.
discoveries.what_confirms: Builder customer concentration commentary in earnings call shows volume softness across DHI/LEN/PHM; gross margin compression beyond cyclical norms.
discoveries.what_kills: Earnings actually beat and selloff is purely sector-driven; insider buying emerges; M&A pipeline commentary remains strong.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Quality compounders moving sharply on earnings need granular segment analysis before fading — IBP-class names often present asymmetric entries 5-10 trading days post-print.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: 2.02 filing dated 5/7 explains the 5-day cumulative; today's incremental drop maps to housing-data print.
research_pointers: Pull IBP earnings transcript — same-branch sales and price/mix
Compare to TopBuild (BLD) reaction
Check builder backlog commentary from recent DHI/LEN prints

discoveries.name: Hecla Mining
discoveries.thesis: CNBC notes silver surged 7% to highest since March on AI/parabolic rally narrative. Hecla is the largest US primary silver producer — operating leverage on a 7% silver move easily justifies +11%. Earnings filed 5/5 likely already digested. This is rational commodity beta with a recent positive print as the floor.
discoveries.what_confirms: Silver holds above current level over next 2 sessions; PAAS, FSM, MAG show similar moves.
discoveries.what_kills: Silver futures reverse on profit-taking; HL 5-day gain is already +17.6%, so positioning getting crowded.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Silver rally headline (CNBC) plus prior positive 2.02 earnings — operating leverage to commodity move is the mechanism.
research_pointers: Check silver ETF (SLV) flows and futures positioning (COT)
Compare HL move to PAAS, MAG, AG — sympathy peers
Watch for follow-through Tuesday on silver

discoveries.name: Dropbox
discoveries.setup: Delayed post-earnings selloff (4 days after print)
discoveries.thesis: DBX reported 5/7 and 5-day change is +5.6% — meaning the stock initially rallied on earnings, but today is giving back -8.3%. This pattern (rally then fade) often indicates either sell-side downgrade following deeper-dive, or a guidance footnote investors initially missed. Worth investigating because the inversion of initial reaction is unusual.
discoveries.what_confirms: Sell-side note today downgrading on net retention or AI competition; specific guide-down line item in earnings supplement.
discoveries.what_kills: Move is just broad SaaS weakness sympathy with no DBX-specific re-read; bounces back tomorrow.
discoveries.what_to_learn: When a stock is positive 5-day but down sharply today after recent earnings, the delayed selloff is often more informative than the initial reaction — the marginal seller has done the work.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: 2.02 filed 5/7 but 5-day change is positive — today's -8% is the inversion that needs explanation.
research_pointers: Check for analyst notes published today on DBX
Review FCF guide and AI-product (Dash) revenue contribution
Compare to BOX, NTAP for storage/collab sympathy

discoveries.name: Celsius Holdings
discoveries.setup: Delayed post-earnings continuation with sector weakness (XLP -0.96%)
discoveries.thesis: Reported 5/7. -8.7% over 5 days with another -5% today, on 2.26x volume. Likely combination of post-earnings drift and consumer staples weakness (XLP and ELF also down). Magnitude consistent with growth-name multiple compression on any whiff of distribution slowdown.
discoveries.what_confirms: Nielsen scanner data shows decelerating Celsius units; PEP distribution commentary softens; further analyst downgrades.
discoveries.what_kills: Scanner data shows reaccel; PEP reaffirms support; Alani Nu integration commentary is positive.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: 2.02 filing 5/7 and concurrent XLP weakness; ELF also down -9% in same complex.
research_pointers: Pull most recent Nielsen / Circana energy drink data
Cross-check with MNST (Monster) reaction
Inventory destocking commentary in CELH transcript

discoveries.name: e.l.f. Beauty
discoveries.thesis: ELF reports in 9 days. -9% on 2.5x volume with no 8-K is pre-earnings de-risking, possibly tied to weakening Nielsen/Circana data in beauty or sympathy with broader consumer weakness. Magnitude is large for pre-positioning unless someone has a channel-check note out today.
discoveries.what_confirms: Sell-side note today citing scanner-data slowdown; sympathy peer (ULTA, COTY) also down sharply; short interest creeping up.
discoveries.what_kills: Buyers step in pre-print; sector rebounds; no specific channel-check note surfaces.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Pre-earnings 9%+ drops within 2 weeks of a print often reflect informed positioning — either channel-check data leaks or known analyst notes; the print itself is then often a 'sell the news avoided' setup.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty filings, only upcoming_earnings 5/20; magnitude exceeds typical pre-earnings drift.
research_pointers: Check for analyst channel-check notes today
Compare to ULTA, COTY, EL price action
Track ELF social-media sentiment / Hyram-style influencer signals

discoveries.name: Ralliant Corporation
discoveries.setup: Pre-earnings ramp into next-day pre-market print
discoveries.thesis: RAL reports 5/12 pre-market — tomorrow morning. +4.9% on near-3x volume into a print is aggressive positioning. 5-day +8.8%. Either leaked positive whisper or sympathy with semi/electronic-components strength (SMH +1.7%, VICR +22%). Asymmetric setup: positioning into a print at near 52-week extension is a fade candidate if guide is anything less than blowout.
discoveries.what_confirms: Tomorrow's print beats and guides up — confirms informed flow.
discoveries.what_kills: In-line print causes immediate -5% gap; or VICR-style rally is just a sympathy halo that fades.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Pre-earnings rallies of 5%+ on 2.5x+ volume into next-session prints create asymmetric fade setups regardless of sector tape — the bar gets raised faster than fundamentals can match.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: upcoming_earnings tomorrow pre-market; no filings; SMH/VICR strength provides sector context.
research_pointers: Read RAL prior-quarter guide and Street consensus
Check options IV — is event being priced fully?
Compare to Fortive (parent spin context) for read-across

discoveries.name: Insight Enterprises
discoveries.thesis: Reported 5/7. 5-day +25.2% — print was clearly a beat. Today's +5% is continuation, plausibly tied to enterprise AI adoption narrative (OpenAI Dresser commentary today). NSIT is an IT solutions/reseller play with direct AI infrastructure exposure. Magnitude rational but momentum is extended.
discoveries.what_confirms: Analyst upward EPS revisions continue; CDW, ARW peers show similar bid.
discoveries.what_kills: Extension into low-float momentum chase fades; gross margin in transcript turns out worse than headline EPS.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: 2.02 5/7 with +25% 5-day — print was the catalyst; today is follow-through.
research_pointers: Compare to CDW reaction
Check gross margin and services mix in transcript
Watch for sell-side PT raises tomorrow

discoveries.name: Kodiak Gas Services
discoveries.thesis: Earnings filed today (5/11). Stock +8.4% on 2x volume into a strong energy tape (Hormuz commentary, oil concerns). Combination of an in-the-day 2.02 print plus +XLE makes the magnitude reasonable. 5-day +10.9% suggests pre-positioning was modest, so post-print rally is real reaction.
discoveries.what_confirms: Compression demand commentary in transcript is strong; contract renewals at higher rates; Permian gas associated volumes commentary positive.
discoveries.what_kills: Energy unwind on Iran ceasefire revival; secondary equity offering announcement.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Today's 2.02 8-K plus sector strength — direct attribution.
research_pointers: Compression contract pricing and utilization in transcript
Compare to USAC, AROC peers
Distribution coverage given energy infrastructure yield demand

discoveries.name: Hawaiian Electric
discoveries.thesis: Reported 5/8 (2.02). -6% today on 2.76x volume despite XLU +0.94% — meaningful underperformance vs sector. HE still carries Maui wildfire litigation overhang; earnings likely surfaced reserve/liability commentary that re-rates the credit. Magnitude justified if filing has updated reserves disclosure.
discoveries.what_confirms: Earnings deck shows expanded wildfire-related reserves or new claimant disclosures; rating agency negative action.
discoveries.what_kills: Reaction is purely a soft EPS print with no incremental litigation news — bounces in days.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: 2.02 filing 5/8 plus underperformance vs XLU +0.94% — utility-relative weakness suggests company-specific issue.
research_pointers: Read 10-Q for updated wildfire litigation reserve commentary
Check PCG playbook for analogue post-fire utility trajectory
Settlement-fund funding mechanism status with State of Hawaii

discoveries.name: Gap, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Sharp move with no 8-K and no scheduled earnings
discoveries.thesis: GAP -7% on 2x volume with empty catalyst_signals. Could be tariff-related (Trump tariff Truth Social posts, apparel imports), pre-earnings de-risking (typically reports late May), or sympathy with other retail weakness. Without a specific catalyst, magnitude is hard to validate.
discoveries.what_confirms: Tariff headlines specifically targeting apparel imports; ANF, AEO, URBN show similar moves; analyst note today.
discoveries.what_kills: No follow-on news, bounces tomorrow — was just a positioning trade.
discoveries.catalyst: Possibly tariff/apparel concerns or unknown channel data
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals; only contextual link is Trump tariff Truth Social posts referencing Supreme Court ruling.
research_pointers: Cross-check apparel peers ANF, AEO, URBN, LEVI
Check Old Navy / Banana Republic channel data
Tariff exposure on China/Vietnam sourcing

catalyst_chains.primary_event: April home sales disappointment + elevated mortgage rates
Silver +7% to multi-month high on AI/parabolic narrative
Hormuz closure persisting, oil normalization pushed to 2027 per Aramco

trump_signals.post_summary: Trump attacks SCOTUS tariff ruling, claims $159B owed back; suggests alternative tariff path will be 'slower and more laborious'
trump_signals.note: Apparel importers (GAP), appliance makers with import exposure (WHR), and beauty importers (ELF) all sit at intersection of tariff policy uncertainty. Trump signaling a slower replacement tariff process is marginally positive for these names short-term, but his rhetoric also raises probability of executive workarounds. Net: tariff policy is in flux and creates two-way risk for the import-exposed consumer complex; mid-cap consumer names with China/Asia sourcing should be monitored for tariff-related headline volatility.

ai_announcements.summary: Digg relaunch as AI news aggregator - no direct public company impact.

ai_announcements.headline: Cowboy Space raises $275M for orbital data centers
ai_announcements.summary: Private company funding for space-based AI data centers. Tangential read-through to launch providers and terrestrial data center power/cooling names, but no listed stock in today's movers maps cleanly.

ai_announcements.headline: Whisper-filled office of the future
ai_announcements.summary: Speculative piece on voice-driven work. No actionable stock-level signal.

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: Today's AI news flow is thin and non-specific (Digg relaunch, space DC funding round, voice-UI speculation). None of the large movers in market_data appear to be moving on AI announcements: ELF, SHAK, WHR, IBP, CELH, LMAT, DBX, HE, GAP, RDNT, AMTM, AGO, PTCT, KGS, SOLS, NSIT all have earnings 8-Ks within the last week — these moves are earnings-driven, not AI-driven. VICR +22% and HL +11% look idiosyncratic (Vicor possibly power-component AI-adjacent rumor, but no announcement in the feed to pin it to). RAL into earnings tomorrow. No AI-overreaction trades to flag with confidence tonight. Quiet night for the AI-selloff thesis.
